title: The One with the Football

jokes.text: No, you want to put them in concentric circles. I want to do this.
jokes.setup: Monica gives Rachel specific instructions on marshmallow placement
jokes.punchline: Rachel rebels against the micro-management in the most petty way possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Rachel's resistance to Monica's control freak tendencies, delivered with just the right amount of defiance

jokes.text: Every year.
jokes.setup: The marshmallow argument between Monica and Rachel
jokes.punchline: Deadpan acknowledgment that this petty conflict is an annual tradition
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical line that captures the repetitive nature of holiday family dynamics

jokes.text: I wanted to wear my bathrobe and eat peanut clusters all day.
jokes.setup: Joey says Chandler never does anything since breaking up with Janice
jokes.punchline: Chandler's pathetic post-breakup goals are hilariously specific and depressing
jokes.explanation: Specific, pathetic details make this funnier than generic 'I'm depressed' - the peanut clusters add perfect specificity

jokes.text: And start drinking in the morning. Don't say I don't have goals!
jokes.setup: Chandler already listed his sad post-breakup routine
jokes.punchline: He escalates to alcoholism but frames it as having ambition
jokes.explanation: The ironic pride in having terrible goals is perfectly Chandler, and the escalation from peanut clusters to morning drinking is expertly timed

jokes.text: Says who? Your mom? Ha-ha-ha. Yes.
jokes.setup: Someone mockingly asks if their mom banned them from football
jokes.punchline: Ross deadpan confirms the juvenile reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - we expect Ross to deny it, but he sheepishly admits the embarrassing truth

jokes.text: No, no, no. You say that proudly!
jokes.setup: Ross mentions 'The Geller Bowl' sheepishly
jokes.punchline: Someone insists he should take pride in the ridiculous family tradition
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that highlights the absurdity of taking family football this seriously

jokes.text: She saw I was about to tag her, so she threw her big fat grandma-arm elbow in my face and just kept running.
jokes.setup: Monica claims she accidentally broke Ross's nose
jokes.punchline: Ross's detailed, bitter description of Monica's deliberate violence
jokes.explanation: 'Big fat grandma-arm elbow' is hilariously specific sibling insult, and the casual 'just kept running' shows Monica's ruthlessness

jokes.text: The touchdown didn't count because of the spectacularly illegal, and savage, nose-breaking.
jokes.setup: Monica claims she won with the touchdown
jokes.punchline: Ross's overly dramatic legal terminology for playground football
jokes.explanation: 'Spectacularly illegal and savage' is wonderfully overwrought for family football, shows Ross taking this way too seriously

jokes.text: And was the curse lifted?
jokes.setup: Dad threw the trophy in the lake to end the fighting
jokes.punchline: Treating the family football conflict like a mythological curse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to fantasy/fairy tale tropes, perfectly economical, elevates the family drama to mythic proportions

jokes.text: Wuss!
jokes.setup: Ross tries to have a private conversation with Monica
jokes.punchline: Monica immediately calls him out publicly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - competitive and direct, cutting through Ross's attempt at discretion

jokes.text: Wait. How we gonna get there? My mom won't let me cross the street.
jokes.setup: They're going outside to play football
jokes.punchline: Adult reveals they still have childish restrictions
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation of the 'mom won't let us play' theme - takes it to an absurd extreme

jokes.text: That almost hit me in the face!
jokes.setup: Someone throws Rachel the football
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dramatic overreaction to a gentle toss
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's inexperience with sports, but fairly predictable physical comedy

jokes.text: And then 'Tennilles.'
jokes.setup: They need to pick captains
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical reference that sounds like it should mean something
jokes.explanation: Absurd non-sequitur that doesn't quite land - feels random rather than clever

jokes.text: Why don't we just bunny up? What? Bunny! Bunny!
jokes.setup: They need a method to pick captains
jokes.punchline: Made-up process that makes no sense but everyone goes along with
jokes.explanation: Funny made-up word that sounds official but meaningless - good absurdist humor

jokes.text: I'm your best friend. Sweetie, don't worry, you'll get picked.
jokes.setup: Rachel expects to be picked by her best friend Monica
jokes.punchline: Monica picks someone else and patronizes Rachel
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - competitive drive overrides friendship, then condescending comfort

jokes.text: You don't pick me. You're stuck with me!
jokes.setup: Monica finally 'picks' Rachel last
jokes.punchline: Rachel correctly identifies she wasn't chosen, she was leftover
jokes.explanation: Rachel's self-aware reaction to being picked last - good mix of hurt and humor

jokes.text: Wow, just like in the pros.
jokes.setup: Monica mentions they have 28 minutes before she has to baste again
jokes.punchline: Comparing their time limit to professional football
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the intersection of football and cooking obligations

jokes.text: So what do you guys really think of Chandler?
jokes.setup: The huddle is supposed to be about football strategy
jokes.punchline: Instead it becomes gossip about teammates
jokes.explanation: Smart misdirection - subverts expectation of football strategy for relationship drama

jokes.text: I thought you knew what you were doing. I thought you meant in life!
jokes.setup: Joey asks if Phoebe knows what she's doing (at football)
jokes.punchline: Phoebe thought he was asking about life in general
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - her misunderstanding reveals deeper philosophical confusion that's both funny and endearing

jokes.text: The ball is Janice. The ball is Janice.
jokes.setup: Chandler is trying to get motivated for football
jokes.punchline: He psychs himself up by imagining the ball is his ex
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Chandler's mental state and gives him motivation, character-specific humor

jokes.text: No, no, actually, losers rhyme.
jokes.setup: Someone says 'Losers walk. Yeah? Losers talk.'
jokes.punchline: Pointing out that the comeback attempt actually proved their point
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke that turns the rhyming comeback against itself - smart wordplay

jokes.text: Great. Now the score's seven to almost seven.
jokes.setup: Phoebe celebrates almost catching a pass
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically treating an incomplete pass like a near-score
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response to participation trophy mentality - economical and cutting

jokes.text: Until we start to look very small.
jokes.setup: Someone asks how long to go long
jokes.punchline: Hilariously literal and impractical instruction
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd instruction that's both literal and completely impractical - great visual comedy

jokes.text: Wow, you're, like, from a whole other country!
jokes.setup: Meeting someone from the Netherlands
jokes.punchline: Stating the obvious with misplaced amazement
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing American ignorance but fairly predictable humor

jokes.text: Her name is Dutch and also 'Margham.'
jokes.setup: Joey tries to remember the Dutch girl's name
jokes.punchline: Joey confuses her nationality with her name and still gets the name wrong
jokes.explanation: Good Joey moment showing his confusion, but the mispronunciation isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: It just amazes me that you're still pulling stuff like this.
jokes.setup: Simple disagreement about what down it is
jokes.punchline: Monica treats a minor football dispute like a character flaw
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica - making everything personal and competitive, escalating minor disagreements

jokes.text: I heard that. Well, I said it loud.
jokes.setup: Ross makes a sarcastic comment under his breath
jokes.punchline: Monica's matter-of-fact response when called out
jokes.explanation: Monica's direct honesty is funnier than being defensive - good character moment

jokes.text: Thanks, man, but it makes a stronger statement if I ask for it myself.
jokes.setup: Chandler offers to get the Dutch girl's number for Joey
jokes.punchline: Joey misunderstands and thinks Chandler was offering to ask for Chandler
jokes.explanation: Good Joey confusion moment - shows his self-absorption and misunderstanding

jokes.text: Math? You're giving me math?
jokes.setup: Joey says Chandler is better with numbers and stuff
jokes.punchline: Chandler's indignation at being relegated to the nerd category
jokes.explanation: Chandler's wounded pride at being seen as the math guy rather than the ladies' man

jokes.text: Well, now you have two.
jokes.setup: Chandler complains about his torn favorite jersey
jokes.punchline: Monica's practical response to his complaint
jokes.explanation: Monica's matter-of-fact response to damage she caused - shows her competitive ruthlessness

jokes.text: Hey, I am good at math!
jokes.setup: Previous argument about Chandler being relegated to math
jokes.punchline: Chandler's delayed, defensive realization
jokes.explanation: Delayed reaction showing Chandler processing the insult - mild callback humor

jokes.text: I've been preparing for that my entire life!
jokes.setup: Joey threatens to make Chandler feel bad about himself
jokes.punchline: Chandler's self-aware acknowledgment of his low self-esteem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler - turns self-deprecation into a defensive weapon, very quotable

jokes.text: Broken boobs! Ow.
jokes.setup: Phoebe gets tackled hard
jokes.punchline: Her unique way of describing the injury
jokes.explanation: Only Phoebe would describe injury this way - perfectly in character and memorably absurd

jokes.text: Cheater, cheater, compulsive eater.
jokes.setup: Traditional playground taunt setup
jokes.punchline: Monica's food-obsessed variation
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific variation on classic playground taunt - very Monica

jokes.text: Dead leg!
jokes.setup: Monica claims to have matured beyond childish behavior
jokes.punchline: Immediately delivers a dead leg
jokes.explanation: Perfect hypocrisy - Monica's actions immediately contradict her words, great physical timing

jokes.text: You know I had swollen glands!
jokes.setup: Monica mentions Ross might have had mono
jokes.punchline: Ross's indignant correction of his medical condition
jokes.explanation: Ross's need to correct even embarrassing medical details - shows his pedantic nature

jokes.text: Is everybody else seeing a troll doll nailed to a two-by-four? Mm-hm. Okay, good.
jokes.setup: The Geller Cup is revealed as this magnificent trophy
jokes.punchline: Chandler's accurate description of the pathetic reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of the trophy's importance with hilariously accurate description

jokes.text: When you went to the hospital for your nose, I swam into the lake and fished it out.
jokes.setup: How did Monica get the trophy back from the lake?
jokes.punchline: Monica's dedication was so extreme she went swimming after it
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's obsessive competitiveness - she literally dove into a lake for this ugly trophy

jokes.text: Tackled by a girl! Bet you don't see that every day.
jokes.setup: Chandler gets tackled by Monica
jokes.punchline: Mock celebration of gender role reversal
jokes.explanation: Dated gender humor that doesn't add much - fairly predictable

jokes.text: Like the turkey, Ross is done!
jokes.setup: They're winning big against Ross's team
jokes.punchline: Monica's Thanksgiving-themed trash talk
jokes.explanation: Good holiday-themed trash talk that fits the Thanksgiving setting and Monica's competitive nature

jokes.text: Oh, what a great argument. Exhaling!
jokes.setup: Monica makes a dismissive 'pfft' sound
jokes.punchline: Ross's overly intellectual analysis of her dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross - turning a simple dismissive sound into an intellectual critique

jokes.text: The Netherlands is this make-believe place where Peter Pan and Tinker Bell come from.
jokes.setup: Chandler tries to show off his geography knowledge
jokes.punchline: Confuses Netherlands with Neverland
jokes.explanation: Funny geographical confusion that sounds confident but is completely wrong

jokes.text: Enough 'geography for the insane.' Let's play ball.
jokes.setup: The ridiculous geography discussion
jokes.punchline: Perfect summarizing dismissal
jokes.explanation: 'Geography for the insane' perfectly captures the absurdity of the previous exchange

jokes.text: Yeah, for dating, general merriment, taking back to your windmill?
jokes.setup: Asking the Dutch girl who she likes
jokes.punchline: Chandler's Dutch stereotypes get increasingly ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Stereotypical humor that escalates well but relies on easy cultural clichés

jokes.text: Why don't you have Captain Hook explain it to her?
jokes.setup: Chandler's confused geography lesson
jokes.punchline: Joey continues the Peter Pan reference
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the Peter Pan joke logically

jokes.text: You hear that? That is her truth, Mr. 'I'll Let You Have Her'!
jokes.setup: The Dutch girl chooses Chandler
jokes.punchline: Chandler's triumphant callback to Joey's earlier condescension
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier setup - Chandler gets to throw Joey's words back at him

jokes.text: I win! You suck! I rule all! Mini-wave in celebration of me!
jokes.setup: Chandler's victory over Joey
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous celebration including physical gesture
jokes.explanation: Chandler's rare moment of triumph leads to over-the-top celebration

jokes.text: I now find you shallow, and, um... a dork.
jokes.setup: Chandler celebrates his victory
jokes.punchline: The Dutch girl changes her mind based on his behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect comeuppance - Chandler's victory celebration immediately backfires

jokes.text: You just saved yourself a couple months of sex.
jokes.setup: The Dutch girl rejects both of them
jokes.punchline: Joey's cynical silver lining
jokes.explanation: Joey's practical way of looking at romantic rejection - darkly funny

jokes.text: From now on, I get the dates, and you stay home on Saturday nights watching Ready, Set, Cook!
jokes.setup: Chandler still thinks he won despite being rejected
jokes.punchline: His specific vision of Joey's loser future
jokes.explanation: The specific show reference makes it funnier than generic 'staying home' - shows period humor

jokes.text: Come on, you hairy-backed Marys!
jokes.setup: Monica trying to motivate her team
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and aggressive trash talk
jokes.explanation: 'Hairy-backed Marys' is wonderfully specific and weird trash talk that only Monica would use

jokes.text: Oh, my God. You dated someone with a glass eye too?
jokes.setup: Speech about guys keeping one eye on TV
jokes.punchline: Literal misinterpretation of the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - takes a metaphor literally in a funny way

jokes.text: The important thing is the Dutch girl picked me! Me, not you! Holland loves Chandler! Thank you, Amsterdam! Good night!
jokes.setup: Someone says winning isn't important
jokes.punchline: Chandler escalates to international romantic triumph
jokes.explanation: Chandler's grandiose delusion about his international appeal, ignoring that she rejected him

jokes.text: Isn't there a 'national football league'? Yes, there is. They play on Sundays and Monday nights. Oh, shoot! I work Monday nights.
jokes.setup: Looking for a football league to join
jokes.punchline: Rachel's naive response to the NFL suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Rachel moment - her obliviousness to how professional sports work

jokes.text: I don't know. I guess the other people just don't care enough.
jokes.setup: Why are they always the ones left holding the ball?
jokes.punchline: Suggesting everyone else lacks their dedication
jokes.explanation: Good ironic take on their obsessive behavior - frames stubbornness as virtue

tmdb_overview: Emotions become the feast of the day when the gang plays a game of touch football on Thanksgiving. Joey and Chandler argue over who gets to date a model.
